findings.title: Near-identical sse/http cases in createTransport switch
findings.description: The 'sse' and 'http' cases are structurally identical: both guard on !config.url, both return { type, url, headers? }. The only difference is the type literal string. This is a near-duplicate block (~9 lines each, 18 total) that could be collapsed.

findings.title: Duplicate url/command validation in store and transport factory
findings.description: The same transport-specific validation (stdio requires command, sse/http requires url) is performed in both McpServerStore.validateInput (lines 142-158) and createTransport (lines 13, 24, 34). This means every server that is added and then connected goes through the same validation twice in two different locations. If the validation rules evolve, both must be updated in lockstep.

findings.title: Merge sse/http validation branches in validateInput
findings.description: Two consecutive if-statements check the exact same condition (!input.url) for two transport types that share the same requirement. These can be merged into a single conditional.

positive_observations: All functions are short (< 40 lines), well within acceptable complexity thresholds
Consistent use of early returns and guard clauses throughout all four files
Clean separation of concerns: store (persistence), transport-factory (creation), client-manager (lifecycle), hub (facade)
Maximum nesting depth is 2 levels across all files, well below complexity thresholds
Cyclomatic complexity stays low (1-5) for all functions
filterUndefined helper is a clean pure function extracted appropriately
